name: Fernbank After Dark
description: Adults-only (21+) evening events with themed science experiences, cocktails, live music, and after-hours museum access. Monthly series — each event has a unique theme (e.g., Cocktails & the Cosmos, Love Lab, The Big Chill).

name: Sips Under the Sea
description: Romantic after-hours aquarium experience (21+) with cocktails, tapas, live music, and access to marine exhibits. Monthly on select evenings.

name: Dinner Detective Murder Mystery
description: Interactive comedy crime-solving dinner show. Full meal included, prizes for top sleuth. Great for date nights or group outings.

name: Al Capone's Speakeasy Escape Room
description: 1920s gangster-themed escape game with an in-room actor bartender. Players take on roles (singer/police). Up to 14 players, 1 hour.

name: Pigalle Cabaret & Speakeasy
address: Underground Atlanta, 50 Alabama St SW, Atlanta, GA 30303
description: Bohemian Paris-themed theater with burlesque, jazz, absinthe service, and rotating themed shows (Barbie Ball, Haunted Tavern, Tiki Thursdays). Recurring weekly shows.

name: High Museum of Art – Friday Jazz
description: Live jazz performances in the museum galleries on select Friday evenings. Combines art and live music in a sophisticated setting.

name: Atlanta BeltLine Cocktails & Bites Tour
description: Guided walk along the BeltLine with craft cocktail tastings and food bites from local spots. Evening tour format.

name: Skyline Park at Ponce City Market
address: 675 Ponce de Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30308
description: Rooftop amusement park with mini golf, carnival games, carnival food, beer garden, slides, and skyline views. Seasonal skating rink in winter.

name: Sloomoo Institute Atlanta
address: Ponce City Market, 675 Ponce de Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30308
description: High-sensory interactive slime museum. Great for kids and adults. Multiple slime stations, satisfying ASMR experiences.

name: LEGO Discovery Center Atlanta
description: Indoor LEGO theme park with rides, building zones, 4D cinema, and miniature Atlanta cityscape. Best for rainy days and young children (ages 3–10 sweet spot).

name: Center for Puppetry Arts
description: Interactive workshops, performances, and a global puppet museum. Shows rotate seasonally but venue is always open. Drop-in Create-A-Puppet workshops run year-round on weekends.
schedule: Tues–Fri 10am, Sat–Sun 11am & 1pm; workshops Sat–Sun after shows

name: Chattahoochee Nature Center
description: Wildlife encounters, raptor center, easy boardwalks through wetlands and forest. Great for toddlers and kids. Memberships available.

name: Sol Dance / Ecstatic Dance Atlanta
description: High-energy non-verbal ecstatic dance experience. Community-oriented, no alcohol, no phones. Bi-weekly Sunday pattern.

name: Atlanta Botanical Garden – General Admission
description: World-class botanical garden with Japanese Garden, Fuqua Orchid Center, children's garden, and seasonal events (Garden Lights in Dec, Orchid Daze in March). Member early access to Japanese Garden for quiet morning visits.

name: Lake Claire Land Trust Drum Circle
address: Lake Claire Community Land Trust, Atlanta, GA 30307
description: Full moon drum circle in a community forest. High community vibe, free, all-ages. Happens monthly on full moon evenings.

name: Axe Throwing at The Battery Atlanta
description: Social axe throwing experience near Truist Park. High energy, great for groups and competitive dates.

name: Indoor Rock Climbing – The Overlook at Stone Summit
description: Indoor rock climbing and bouldering gym. Physical reset activity, great for cold weather. Day passes available.

name: Tubing the Chattahoochee
description: Float the Chattahoochee River on inner tubes. Low effort, high relaxation. Multiple outfitters near Atlanta. Best May–September.

name: High Museum of Art – UPS Second Sundays
description: Free admission on the second Sunday of each month with special family programming. One of the best free museum days in Atlanta.

name: Emory Carlos Museum
address: 571 S Kilgo Cir NE, Atlanta, GA 30322
venue: Michael C. Carlos Museum at Emory
description: Intimate, quiet museum of ancient arts on Emory's campus. Collections span Egypt, Greece, Rome, the Americas, and sub-Saharan Africa. Free to enter.

name: Stone Mountain Park
address: 1000 Robert E Lee Blvd, Stone Mountain, GA 30083
description: Major family attraction with SkyLift chairlift to summit, historic scenic railroad, Treetop Challenge ropes course, and nightly Lasershow Spectacular (March–Oct). Train is a hit for toddlers.
price_range: $12–30+ depending on activities; parking fee applies

name: Zoo Atlanta
description: Home to giant pandas, gorillas, Komodo dragons, and more. Keeper talks daily. Carousel and Zoo Train are toddler favorites. Member nights run periodically.

name: Georgia Aquarium
description: One of the world's largest aquariums. Whale sharks, beluga whales, manta rays. 4D theater, dive shows. ATL Botanical Garden membership holders get discounts.

name: Children's Museum of Atlanta
address: 275 Centennial Olympic Park Dr NW, Atlanta, GA 30313
description: Interactive exhibits covering food, engineering, and arts for young kids. Toddler Saturday (first Saturday) is specially designed for ages 1–3. Permanent exhibits include Fundamentally Food and Tools for Solutions.

name: Fernbank Museum – WildWoods & Giant Screen Theater
description: 75-acre forest preserve with trails adjacent to the museum. IMAX-style Giant Screen Theater shows nature and science films. Sensing Nature exhibit is hands-on for kids. Included with or add-on to museum admission.

name: Dad's Garage Theatre
description: Atlanta's top improv and comedy theater. Family shows (Wowie Zowie, Tight Acquaintances D&D improv) on select Saturdays/Sundays. Adult shows and workshops ongoing. Family Improv Workshop on first Saturdays.
schedule: Select Saturdays/Sundays for family; adult shows ongoing

name: Laughing Skull Lounge
description: Atlanta's dedicated stand-up comedy club inside Vortex Bar. Intimate venue with shows most nights. Regular lineups plus touring national acts.

name: Punchline Comedy Club
address: 280 Hilderbrand Dr NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328
description: Atlanta's longest-running comedy club with national headliners and full dinner service. 2-drink minimum.

name: Terminal West
address: 887 W Marietta St NW, Atlanta, GA 30318
description: Top mid-size music venue in Atlanta. Intimate standing floor with great acoustics. Primary home for electronic, indie, and alternative acts. Check calendar for upcoming shows.

name: The Eastern
description: Premier live music venue in Little Five Points. Converted theater with excellent sound and sightlines. Strong electronic and indie programming.

name: Variety Playhouse
description: Classic all-ages venue in Little Five Points. Excellent for downtempo, folk, world music, and indie acts. One of Atlanta's best mid-size rooms.

name: Fox Theatre
description: Atlanta's landmark grand theater. Hosts Broadway touring shows, ballet, concerts, and film screenings. Moorish architectural interior is spectacular.

name: Alliance Theatre
description: Atlanta's leading professional theater company. Produces scripted plays and world premieres. High intellectual caliber. Season runs Sept–June.

name: Atlanta Botanical Garden – Garden Lights Holiday Nights
description: Massive holiday light installation transforming the gardens each November–January. One of Atlanta's signature seasonal events. Book tickets early — sells out.

name: Atlanta Jazz Festival
address: Piedmont Park, 400 Park Dr NE, Atlanta, GA 30306
description: Free outdoor jazz festival in Piedmont Park over Memorial Day weekend. One of the largest free jazz festivals in the US. Family-friendly.

name: Inman Park Festival & Tour of Homes
description: Annual neighborhood festival in late April with a parade, artisan market, live music, food, and tour of historic Victorian homes. Free admission.

name: Atlanta Dogwood Festival
address: Piedmont Park, 400 Park Dr NE, Atlanta, GA 30306
description: Fine arts festival in Piedmont Park each April coinciding with dogwood blooms. Artist market, live entertainment, food vendors. Free.

name: Atlanta Botanical Garden – Orchid Daze
description: Annual tropical orchid immersion in the Fuqua Conservatory each February–March. Thousands of orchids in a rainforest setting.
price_range: Included with admission; free with membership

name: St. Patrick's Day Parade – Midtown Atlanta
address: Peachtree St NW, Midtown Atlanta, GA
description: One of the largest St. Patrick's Day parades in the Southeast. Midtown streets fill with floats, bagpipes, and community groups. Free to watch.
schedule: March (Saturday before or on St. Patrick's Day)

name: Dragon Con Parade
description: Massive cosplay parade down Peachtree Street on Labor Day Saturday morning. Free to watch from sidewalk. Part of Dragon Con convention.

name: Summer Movie Series at Atlantic Station
address: Atlantic Station, 1380 Atlantic Dr NW, Atlanta, GA 30363
description: Free outdoor movie screenings in the Atlantic Station plaza during summer evenings. Bring blankets and lawn chairs.

name: Peachtree Road Race Spectating
address: Peachtree Rd NW to Piedmont Park, Atlanta, GA
description: The world's largest 10K. Spectating along the route on July 4th is festive and family-friendly. Grab a spot in Buckhead or Midtown.

name: Virginia-Highland Summerfest
address: North Highland Ave NE, Virginia-Highland, Atlanta, GA 30306
description: Annual neighborhood street festival in June with live music, food, artisan vendors, and a classic car show. One of Atlanta's most beloved neighborhood festivals.

name: Chattahoochee Nature Center – Flying Colors Butterfly Festival
description: Annual butterfly festival in June with butterfly releases, live music, nature activities, and educational programs for kids.

name: District Atlanta
description: Atlanta's premier 21+ nightclub for electronic music. Multi-room venue with world-class DJ bookings (house, techno, melodic). Check calendar for headliners.

name: The Masquerade (Heaven / Hell / Purgatory)
description: Multi-room music venue (Heaven, Hell, Purgatory, Altar) covering multiple genres and capacities. Strong for alternative, metal, electronic, and indie shows.

name: Eddie's Attic
description: Intimate singer-songwriter venue in Decatur. Acoustic, folk, and indie acts in a listening-room setting. Launched John Mayer and other artists. Quiet, attentive crowd.

name: Blind Willie's Blues Club
address: 828 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306
description: Atlanta's legendary blues bar in Virginia-Highland. Live blues nightly. Intimate, no-frills dive bar setting with serious music.

name: The Earl
address: 488 Flat Shoals Ave SE, Atlanta, GA 30316
description: East Atlanta Village's go-to indie rock bar and music venue. Great kitchen, strong local and touring indie bookings. Unpretentious neighborhood feel.

name: Plaza Theatre
address: 1049 Ponce de Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306
description: Atlanta's oldest operating movie theater. Indie film, cult cinema, midnight screenings, and festivals. Essential for art-house film lovers.

name: Piedmont Park
description: Atlanta's central urban park. Walking, cycling, dog park, active oval, tennis. Hosts most of the city's major outdoor festivals. Best on weekends.

name: Atlanta History Center
address: 130 W Paces Ferry Rd NW, Atlanta, GA 30305
description: Sprawling museum campus in Buckhead covering Atlanta and Civil War history. Includes Swan House (historic home), Centennial Olympic Games Museum, and 33 acres of gardens.

name: Illuminarium Atlanta
description: Immersive projection theater experience with floor-to-ceiling 360° visuals and spatial audio. Rotating themes (Safari, Space, etc.). High-impact, photogenic.

name: Candlelight Concerts Series
address: Various Atlanta venues (The Chapel on Sycamore, etc.)
description: Intimate string quartet and ensemble concerts in candlelit architectural spaces. Rotating programs (Beethoven, hip-hop, film scores, etc.). Runs across multiple Atlanta venues.

name: Candle Making Workshop (BYOB)
address: Various locations (Round Trip Brewing, Unity Atlanta Church, etc.)
description: Hands-on BYOB candle-making classes where you blend custom scents. Rotating venues. Wine-friendly atmosphere. Great for date nights.

name: Atlanta BeltLine Trail
address: Atlanta BeltLine, multiple access points, Atlanta, GA
description: 22-mile urban trail loop connecting 45 Atlanta neighborhoods. Walking, running, cycling. Lined with public art, restaurants, and breweries. Eastside Trail (Old Fourth Ward to Inman Park) is the most active section.

name: Ponce City Market
address: 675 Ponce de Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30308
description: Renovated Sears building on the BeltLine. Restaurants, market hall, rooftop Skyline Park, live events. Hub for the neighborhood.
price_range: Free to visit; activities priced separately

name: Fulton County Library Storytimes
address: Various branches (Central, East Point, Southwest, Kirkwood)
description: Free weekly story time programs for babies through preschoolers. Programs include Baby & Me (0–2), Toddler Time (1–3), Little Sprouts (walking toddlers), and Preschool Storytime (3–5).

name: Atlanta Ballet
address: Cobb Energy Performing Arts Centre / Fox Theatre, Atlanta, GA
description: Atlanta's professional ballet company. Season includes The Nutcracker (December), plus contemporary and classical productions. Frida (2026) received strong reviews.

name: Atlanta Symphony Orchestra
description: Grammy Award-winning orchestra. Subscription concerts, pops series, and family matinees. Robert Spano era legacy continues.

name: Chastain Park Amphitheatre (Cadence Bank Amphitheatre)
description: Outdoor amphitheater in Chastain Park known for its BYO wine/food policy. Tables up front, lawn in back. Relaxed, social concert experience. Season runs May–October.

name: Centennial Olympic Park
address: 265 Park Ave W NW, Atlanta, GA 30313
description: Downtown park built for the 1996 Olympics. Fountain of Rings is a must for kids (wades in summer). Free outdoor concerts and events throughout the year.

name: Six Flags Over Georgia
description: Major regional theme park with roller coasters, water park (summer), and seasonal events (Holiday in the Park, Fright Fest). Season passes offer strong value.
schedule: Weekends year-round; daily summers and holidays

name: Museum of Design Atlanta (MODA)
description: The only museum in the Southeast dedicated exclusively to design — architecture, graphic design, fashion, industrial design. Small but curated exhibitions.

name: Believe Music Hall (Iris Presents)
address: 200 Centennial Olympic Park Dr NW, Atlanta, GA 30313
description: Large-capacity electronic music club and event space downtown. Home to Iris Presents productions — major EDM/electronic bookings.

name: Tabernacle Atlanta
description: Beautifully restored historic church turned concert venue. 2,600 capacity standing floor. Strong for larger touring artists across genres.

name: 19hz.info – Underground Electronic Listings
description: Community-maintained listing site for underground electronic music events in Atlanta — techno, house, DnB, experimental. The go-to source for Lunchbox, warehouse, and underground parties.

name: Lunchbox ATL
address: Westside, Atlanta, GA (exact address varies by event)
description: Atlanta's preeminent underground techno and electronic venue. Small capacity, exceptional sound system, serious music programming.

name: Rialto Center for the Arts
description: Georgia State University's performing arts center downtown. World music, jazz, dance, and chamber music in an intimate restored theater.

name: Spivey Hall
description: Acoustically perfect concert hall 20 min south of Atlanta. Presents world-class chamber music, organ recitals, jazz, and international soloists. Considered one of the best acoustic rooms in the US.
